nodes.name: 新西兰岩鹩
nodes.summary: 新西兰岩鹩（New Zealand rock wren，学名 Xenicus gilviventris），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，已关联3个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括气候变化、外来捕食者。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 外来捕食者
nodes.summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

links.key: bird-新西兰岩鹩-0184c36f__distributed_in__loc-南阿爾卑斯山脈-04fb6517
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |title=Rock wren Pīwauwau |url=http://www.nzbirdsonline.org.nz/species/rock-wren |website=New Zealand Birds Online}} of the Southern Alps, the Tasman Mountains of Northwest Nelson, and the Victoria Range of Westland, all in the South Island; it is New Zealand's only truly alpine bird. Subfossil remains suggest before Polynesian settlement it was also found in lowland forest

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |title=Rock wren Pīwauwau |url=http://www.nzbirdsonline.org.nz/species/rock-wren |website=New Zealand Birds Online}} of the Southern Alps, the Tasman Mountains of Northwest Nelson, and the Victoria Range of Westland, all in the South Island; it is New Zealand's only truly alpine bird. Subfossil remains suggest before Polynesian settlement it was also found in lowland forest

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: | year=2018| title= Control of invasive predators improves breeding success of an endangered alpine passerine | journal=Ibis| volume=160| pages= 892–899 | doi=10.1111/ibi.12617| issue=4|s2cid= 91028336 }} The long-term effect of climate change on their alpine habitat is also a threat, as warmer temperatures will allow rats to move higher into the mountains. In 2008–2010, a total of 40 rock wrens were translocated to Secretary Island, an rodent-free island in Fiordland, the third-tallest island in New Zealand. In 2010 a survey located 12 unbanded rock wrens, indicating they were successfully breeding. The rock wren was voted New Zealand Bird of the Year for 2022
